prompt: kolm.ai sells an Agent Security-Review Readiness product: an AI vendor uploads their agent logs and gets a cryptographically signed (Ed25519), offline-verifiable evidence report mapped to SOC2/ISO42001/NIST AI RMF/EU AI Act/OWASP/MITRE that they hand their enterprise buyer's security team. We want to sell it OFF THE SHELF (self-serve, zero sales touch). Research how SOTA bottoms-up security/compliance SaaS (Vanta, Drata, Snyk, Aikido, Socket, Wiz) design SELF-SERVE ONBOARDING in 2026: the exact steps from signup to first value, time-to-first-value targets, the friction-killers (no credit card for trial, instant scan, templated connectors), and what an onboarding MUST have to convert a technical buyer without a demo. Cite specifics (URLs, @handle+date). End with a concrete list of what kolm's onboarding needs to be off-the-shelf.

For an AI-agent security audit product, how do SOTA tools INGEST customer data (agent/LLM logs, traces) in 2026 with the least friction? Compare: file/JSONL upload, REST API, language SDK, CI/CD plugin (GitHub Action), cloud log connectors (CloudWatch/Datadog/LangSmith/Helicone), OpenTelemetry, and MCP server integration. Which ingestion paths do self-serve buyers actually adopt, and which are table-stakes vs nice-to-have? Cite real products + adoption signals. Conclude with the 2-3 ingestion methods kolm should ship to be off-the-shelf (kolm currently accepts JSONL/API upload only).

Enumerate the TABLE-STAKES features a B2B security/compliance SaaS must have off-the-shelf in 2026 so an enterprise buyer does not bounce: SSO/SAML, SCIM, RBAC/teams/roles, audit-log export, REST API + OpenAPI docs, webhooks, status page, SLA, data export/portability, the vendor's own SOC2, in-app + email support. For EACH, mark dealbreaker-if-missing vs expected vs differentiator, with cited evidence (procurement checklists, @handle+date, vendor trust pages). Then list which kolm (a founder-led, self-serve signed-evidence product) must have at minimum to close mid-market deals self-serve.

Research SOTA shareable Trust Center / security-evidence-sharing products in 2026 (SafeBase, Whistic, Vanta Trust Center, Conveyor, Drata Trust Center). kolm gives each customer a shareable signed Trust link their buyer's reviewer verifies offline. What features make a shared trust/evidence page CREDIBLE and REUSABLE by enterprise reviewers: access control / email-gating / NDA click-through, auto-updating evidence, framework crosswalk display, document vault, questionnaire auto-answer, audit trail of who viewed. Cite specifics. Conclude with what kolm's Trust link page should add beyond rendering a signed report to match SOTA trust centers.

What is the 2026 STATE OF THE ART for cryptographically verifiable security/compliance evidence, technically? Cover Sigstore (cosign/Fulcio/Rekor), in-toto attestations, SLSA provenance, C2PA content credentials, RFC 6962 / trillian transparency logs, and Ed25519 vs ECDSA vs PQC signatures. kolm signs reports with Ed25519, offers offline browser verify, two-tier (signature + issuer-keyring provenance), and a Merkle transparency log. What would a sophisticated security buyer or auditor SCRUTINIZE or expect that kolm might lack (key rotation/revocation, timestamping/RFC3161, a public transparency log with inclusion proofs, a documented trust root, reproducibility)? Cite standards/projects. List concrete hardening kolm should add to be credibly SOTA.

What developer experience (DX) do SOTA security/dev-tool products ship in 2026 to be adopted off-the-shelf by technical buyers: typed SDKs (TS/Python), an OpenAPI spec, copy-paste quickstarts, a CLI, idempotent APIs, great error messages, sandbox/test mode, API keys + scoped tokens, rate-limit transparency? Cite exemplary DX (Stripe, Snyk, Resend, Clerk, Modal). kolm has a REST API + a CLI + a node SDK + OpenAPI. Identify the DX gaps that would stop a developer from self-serving (e.g. missing Python SDK, no sandbox mode, thin quickstart) and the top 5 DX improvements that most increase off-the-shelf conversion.

How do SOTA bottoms-up B2B security SaaS package and meter SELF-SERVE billing in 2026? kolm has: free Scan, $750 one-time Signed Report, $299/$999 mo Continuous, $25k named-co-signer Attestation, all flat, Stripe. Research: free-tier limits that convert without cannibalizing, usage/seat/agent metering norms, in-app upgrade UX, card-swipe vs invoice thresholds, annual discounts, the proof-of-value moment placement, and where a self-serve ceiling should hand off to sales. Cite real pricing/packaging (with URLs). Recommend the billing/packaging refinements (NOT price changes - prices are locked) that maximize self-serve conversion: metering, limits enforcement, upgrade prompts, dunning, receipts/invoices.

What is the 2026 SOTA reference STACK for a small-team, multi-tenant B2B security SaaS that must be reliable and sellable: auth (Clerk vs WorkOS vs Auth0 vs custom), billing (Stripe), database (Postgres/Neon/Supabase vs SQLite/JSON-file), hosting (Vercel vs Railway vs Fly vs AWS), background jobs/cron, secrets management, observability (Sentry/OTel), and SSO/SCIM provisioning (WorkOS). kolm currently runs custom API-key auth, a hybrid JSON/SQLite file store on a Railway volume, Express on Railway + static on Vercel, and an in-process scheduler. Critically assess which of kolm's choices are fine vs which are liabilities for selling to enterprises (e.g. file-store durability, custom auth, no managed SSO), and give a pragmatic upgrade path that does not require a rewrite. Cite sources.

Map the 2026 AI-agent-security / LLM-security product category and what enterprise AI-security reviews now REQUIRE: Lakera, Protect AI, HiddenLayer, Robust Intelligence (Cisco), Cyphrex, Calypso, plus agent-specific entrants. For each, what they prove about agent safety (prompt-injection resistance, tool/permission scoping, data egress, runtime guardrails, red-team) and how they package it. What capabilities is an enterprise buyer's 2026 AI-vendor security questionnaire demanding that kolm's audit (least-privilege + audit-trail + injection battery + framework mapping, point-in-time + continuous) does NOT yet cover? Cite @handle+date / URLs. List the capability gaps kolm should close to be a complete off-the-shelf agent-security-evidence product.

How deep and how presented must compliance-framework mapping be for an enterprise reviewer to TRUST a signed security attestation in 2026? kolm maps findings to SOC2 TSC, ISO 42001, NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act Art.12/14, OWASP LLM/Agentic Top 10, MITRE ATLAS. Research what reviewers actually check: control-by-control crosswalk, evidence-per-control, gap/exception handling, scope statements, and how SOTA tools (Vanta, Drata, Secureframe) present a control matrix that a reviewer accepts. Cite specifics. Conclude with how kolm should present its crosswalk (UI + report) to maximize reviewer trust, and any framework coverage gaps to add.

To SELL a security product off-the-shelf, what must the VENDOR's OWN security posture be in 2026? Research the buyer due-diligence checklist a security tool itself must pass: its own SOC2 Type II / ISO 27001, pen-test report, sub-processor list, DPA, data-residency/encryption claims, SSO, vulnerability disclosure policy, bug bounty, incident response. kolm is a Series-A-stage founder-led product with a public site, DPA/BAA/subprocessors pages, and signed-evidence tech but no SOC2 of its own yet. What is the minimum credible vendor-security posture kolm needs to not get blocked in its OWN customers' security reviews, and in what order should it acquire these? Cite sources.

What are the most common reasons a self-serve / off-the-shelf B2B SECURITY product fails to convert or loses the deal in 2026? Find verbatim founder and buyer accounts (@handle+date) and post-mortems: missing SSO, no audit-log export, weak/incomplete docs, no human support option, trust gaps (no SOC2), broken onboarding, no integrations, pricing opacity, slow time-to-value, no sandbox. Rank the deal-killers by frequency/severity for a product like kolm (signed agent-security evidence, founder-led, self-serve with a premium human tier). Conclude with the prioritized anti-patterns kolm must avoid and the single highest-ROI fix to be off-the-shelf sellable.
